title: Binary search with Newton's method sqrt approximation to optimize recent checkpoint lookup
explanation: Standard binary search is O(log n) starting from the middle. Since getPastVotes is almost always called for recent timepoints, starting the search from h - sqrt(h) (near the end) dramatically reduces iterations for the common case. Newton's method computes the integer square root of h in ~6 division iterations, all inline in assembly. For h=1000, this starts at ~968 instead of 500 — typically resolves in 1-2 iterations instead of 10.
gas_saved_unit: per lookup (proportional to array length, saves ~8 SLOADs for 1000-entry array)
when_to_apply: Binary search over append-only sorted arrays where recent entries are queried most often
when_not_to_apply: Arrays queried uniformly across all indices, small arrays under 6 elements where standard binary search is faster
